diffCoverage.notes: Checked the dedupe window logic, false-merge risk, and that distinct messages still persist/render.

findings.evidence: The dedupe is bounded and outcome-specific: it matches only outgoing rows with the same thread + body and a different msg_id within a 15s window (ABS(ts diff) <= 15000), then returns Ok(()) without inserting or bumping counters. It cannot loop and does not block the realtime bridge. The offline test save_message_dedupes_outgoing_echo_across_msg_ids proves both the dedupe and that a distinct outgoing line still persists. The frontend mirror uses the same 15s window and merges in place (adopts the authoritative id), so a later msg-id dedupe stays consistent.
findings.fix: Accepted limitation (echo-dedupe-identical-rapid-sends): two intentional identical sends within 15s collapse to one; a future client correlation id would make reconciliation exact.

summary: Reliability review: the echo dedupe is bounded (outgoing + same thread/body + differing msg_id + 15s window), deterministic, and unit-tested on the store side; it never blocks the bridge and distinct messages still flow. The only trade-off (identical rapid sends collapsing) is recorded as an accepted low risk. No blocking findings; pass.
